universities.name: Constructor University
universities.summary: Constructor University is a private, state-recognised university in Bremen, Germany, with an English-speaking residential campus and students from more than 120 countries. It offers accredited programmes across computer science, engineering, business, natural sciences and social sciences.
universities.campusLifestyle: Constructor University has one residential campus in Bremen with teaching buildings, laboratories, a library, sports and student activity facilities, dining services and four residential colleges. The university describes a community of more than 2,000 students representing over 120 nationalities. Undergraduate students receive academic advising, and student clubs operate across academic, cultural, entrepreneurial and recreational interests.
universities.cityProfile: Bremen is a city-state in northern Germany with established aerospace, automotive, logistics, maritime and technology sectors. The Constructor campus is in Bremen-Nord, around 17 kilometres from the city centre. Students should account for local travel, residence registration, health insurance and the practical value of German language skills outside the English-speaking campus.
universities.practicalExposure: The undergraduate model includes discipline-specific laboratory and project work. Computer Science, Electrical and Computer Engineering and Robotics students use computing and engineering modules before completing internship and career components. Industrial Engineering and Management includes production, logistics, analytics and project modules plus a programme-specific internship. MBA students complete applied projects and thesis work; the two-year route also includes accelerator modules and an optional internship.
universities.hostelOverview: First-year undergraduate students live on campus. The university’s 2026/27 cost sheet lists shared-room housing and a meal plan as billed charges, with a limited number of higher-priced single rooms subject to availability. MBA and other graduate applicants should confirm current housing availability and contract conditions directly because accommodation arrangements can differ by student category.
universities.dietarySupport: Campus dining facilities provide meals and beverages through the billed meal plan for undergraduate residents. Students with dietary, allergy, religious or medical requirements should confirm current menus and accommodation arrangements with the university before enrolment.
universities.safetyOverview: The campus provides a structured residential environment, but students remain responsible for German residence requirements, health insurance and personal safety. Emergency procedures, residence-college rules and support contacts should be checked during orientation. No page should interpret a residential campus as a guarantee of individual safety.
universities.studentSupport: Support includes admissions guidance, academic advising, career services, financing guidance, orientation and residential-college services. The university publishes regional recruitment contacts and application guidance for international applicants. German-language learning is available and is useful for internships, daily life and employment even though the listed programmes are taught in English.
whyChoose: English-taught programmes on one international residential campus
State-recognised institution with accredited bachelor’s and master’s programmes
Engineering curricula include laboratories, projects and mandatory practical experience
Two distinct MBA formats serve different academic and work-experience profiles
thingsToConsider: Private-university tuition and residential charges require a complete funding plan
Scholarships and financing can reduce costs but are not guaranteed
Undergraduate direct-entry eligibility depends on the applicant’s school qualification
German is useful for internships, daily life and employment despite English-medium teaching
bestFitFor: Students seeking an English-speaking residential university in Germany
Applicants comfortable with a selective international admissions process
Engineering students who want interdisciplinary and practical course structures
MBA applicants who meet the relevant degree and work-experience conditions
recognitionBadges: State-recognised by the Free Hanseatic City of Bremen
Programmes accredited under the German Accreditation Council system

faq.question: Is Constructor University public or private?
faq.answer: Constructor University is a private, state-recognised university in Bremen. The university states that it received unlimited state recognition in 2021.

faq.question: Are programmes taught in English?
faq.answer: English is the university’s teaching and research language. The six programmes included here are presented by the university as English-taught programmes.

faq.question: How long are the bachelor’s programmes?
faq.answer: The listed bachelor’s programmes normally require six semesters, or three years, of full-time study and carry 180 ECTS credits.

faq.question: How much is undergraduate tuition?
faq.answer: For 2026/27 the university lists tuition of EUR 10,000 per semester. Housing, meals, university fees, transport and other living costs are additional.

faq.question: Does Constructor University offer more than one MBA?
faq.answer: Yes. It publishes a two-year MBA worth 120 ECTS and a separate one-year MBA worth 60 ECTS. Their entry profiles and work-experience expectations differ.

faq.question: Can international students apply?
faq.answer: The university accepts international applications and publishes visa and no-visa rolling deadlines. Eligibility still depends on the applicant’s qualification, English evidence and programme requirements.

admissionsContent.overview: Applications are submitted through Constructor University’s portal. Requirements depend on study level and programme.

eligibility.intro: Eligibility is assessed from the applicant’s qualification and submitted evidence.
items: Recognised school-leaving qualification for bachelor’s entry
Recognised bachelor’s degree and required work experience for MBA entry
Programme-level English-language evidence or an accepted waiver

admissionSteps: Choose the exact programme and check its current requirements
Prepare identity, academic, English and supporting documents
Review the admission and financing decision
Complete enrolment, funding and visa steps

eligibleAudiences: Applicants with a qualifying secondary-school credential
restrictions: Direct entry depends on German higher-education entrance qualification equivalence

programmes.feeNotes: Tuition is EUR 10,000 per semester for the 2026/27 intake. Housing, meals, mandatory fees, insurance and personal costs are additional.

teachingPhases.details: CHOICE, CORE and CAREER modules with computing foundations, electives, internship or start-up option and bachelor’s thesis.
